revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:温泉マーク]][[ko:온천 기호]][[zh:溫泉記號]]
{{Otheruses|Map symbols|{{ill|List of Urusei Yatsura characters|en|List of Urusei Yatsura characters|qid=Q2302027|lt=Characters in Urusei Yatsura|1=List of Urusei Yatsura characters}}|{{ill|Staff among the characters in Urusei Yatsura|de|Personal unter den Charakteren von Urusei Yatsura|ja|うる星やつらの登場人物#教職員|zh|福星小子的教职员工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Staff among the characters in Urusei Yatsura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Staff among the characters in Urusei Yatsura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Staff among the characters in Urusei Yatsura|1=List of Urusei Yatsura characters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2302027}}}} {{symbol character|♨}} '''Onsen māku''' (温泉マーク, {{big|♨}}) or '''onsen symbol''' is a {{ill|Map symbol|en|Map symbol|qid=Q1752004|lt=map symbol|1=Map symbol}} used on Japanese {{ill|Map|en|Map|qid=Q4006|lt=maps|1=Map}} (including {{ill|Topographic map|en|Topographic map|qid=Q216526|lt=topographic maps|1=Topographic map}}) to indicate the location of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=hot springs|1=Onsen}} and {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|qid=Q1365924|lt=mineral springs|1=Mineral spring}}. It is also used to mark {{ill|Public bathing|en|Public bathing|qid=Q785952|lt=Public Bathhouse|1=Public bathing}}. In the 1:25,000 topographic map schema (Heisei 14), its index number is 125. It corresponds to Unicode code point U+2668 and JIS X 0213 position 1-6-76.
The design differs from the {{ill|Pictogram|en|Pictogram|qid=Q52827|lt=pictogram|1=Pictogram}} for hot springs used by the {{ill|International Organization for Standardization|en|International Organization for Standardization|qid=Q15028|lt=International Organization for Standardization|1=International Organization for Standardization}} (ISO). In 2016, the {{ill|Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry|en|Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry|qid=Q1197264|lt=Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry|1=Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry}} announced a revision to the traditional mark—citing concerns that visitors to the 2020 Tokyo Olympic and Paralympic Games might mistake it for steaming food on a hot plate—but ultimately decided to continue using both designs<ref name="朝日20210731"/>.
== Overview == The onsen symbol first appeared as a {{ill|topographic map symbol|de|topographisches Kartensymbol|ja|地形図図式の記号|zh|地形图符号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/topographic map symbol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/topographic map symbol|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/topographic map symbol|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the provisional 1:20,000 topographic map schema of 1884 (Meiji 17)<ref>(財)日本地図センター『地図記号のうつりかわり -地形図図式・記号の変遷-』1994年（平成6年）3月31日発行, pp.126-127</ref>. This schema was used for the semi-official maps of the Osaka region surveyed by the Army General Staff's Land Survey Department between 1884 and 1890<ref>(財)日本地図センター『地図記号のうつりかわり…』1994年, p.17</ref>. According to research by Kikuzaburō Takagi of the Ministry of Construction's Survey Department (later the {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|qid=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}), the earliest map symbol resembling the onsen mark dates to a dot map established by the Ministry of Home Affairs' Geographical Bureau Survey Division in 1884<ref name="外山2009">{{Sfn|外山健一|2009|pp=2-3}}</ref>.
Since then, it has been used consistently on topographic maps. However, its official name has changed over time: from "温泉" in the 1884–1895 map schema, to "礦泉" from 1900 to 1942, to "温（鉱）泉" from 1955 to 1960, and to "温泉・鉱泉" from 1965 onward.
Although the onsen mark was used from the Meiji era to denote public bathhouses and inns with baths, from around 1950 onwards, so-called "love inns" targeting mixed-gender couples began using it in advertising to improve their image as places of leisure{{sfn|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2012|pp=48-62}}. Because its shape resembles an upside-down jellyfish, it became slangily known as '''sakasa kurage''' ("upside-down jellyfish") and even came to refer to the inns themselves<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://dot.asahi.com/articles/-/110886?page=3|title=「風立ちぬ」が“闇の最高傑作”？ 昭和のエロ映画「ブルーフィルム」の世界|publisher=[[週刊朝日]]|date=2016-12-06|accessdate=2021-11-22}}</ref>.
Eventually, some onsen ryokan associations, concerned about the mark's negative connotations, began removing it from advertisements. Usage of the onsen mark was formally banned for love inns in 1955{{sfn|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2012|pp=48-62}}. By the 1970s, love hotels with Western-style exteriors had replaced traditional inns, and the term sakasa kurage fell out of common use.
Once freed from its erotic associations, the onsen mark became established as a symbol for bathhouses. It continued in use in Taiwan and the Korean Peninsula since Japanese rule, and in South Korea it still denotes inns, hot spring facilities, and public bathhouses.
== Display on topographic maps == On 1:25,000 topographic maps, the onsen symbol marks major {{ill|hot spring sources|de|Heißquellenquellen|ja|温泉および鉱泉の泉源|zh|温泉及矿泉泉源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring sources|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring sources|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring sources|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579085}} as defined by the {{ill|Hot Springs Act|de|Heißquellengesetz|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|zh|温泉法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Springs Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Springs Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Springs Act|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} (Law No.125 of July 10, 1948). The symbol appears at the source's location, but if the source is distant from the bathing facilities, it may also mark the facility. The symbol measures 1.5 mm vertically and horizontally, with a line width of 0.1 mm, printed in black. Its exact point is the midpoint of the symbol's bottom edge.
== Changes in style == The onsen mark consists of a tub and three lines representing steam. While the tub's shape has remained largely unchanged since 1884, the steam lines have varied in style, divided into three major periods since 1895<ref name="zenrin">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://shop.zenrin.co.jp/shop/magazine/walk/091023.jsp|title=地図・測量はじめてものがたり 温泉記号の初め|work=ゼンリンめ〜るまがじんVol.132|publisher=[[ゼンリン]]|date=2009-10-23|accessdate=2016-12-12}}</ref>: # The first period (1895–1917): steam lines are curved. # The second period (1917–2002): steam lines are straight. # The third period (2002 onward): steam lines returned to curves<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://globe.asahi.com/feature/memo/2012051700028.html|title=温泉に歴史あり 地図をつくる|work=[[朝日新聞グローブ]]|publisher=朝日新聞社|date=2012-05-20|accessdate=2016-12-12}}</ref>.
Curved lines were reintroduced because (1) they more accurately represent steam, and (2) the switch to digital map production in 2002 allowed easier rendering of curves<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASFK1700F_X10C13A1000000/|title=東京ふしぎ探検隊 温泉マークの湯気、昔は直線 郵便局は封筒だった|work=NIKKEI STYLE|publisher=[[日本経済新聞社]]、[[日経BP社]]|date=2013-01-18|accessdate=2016-12-12}}</ref>.
During the transition, the steam's direction caused confusion. The 2002 schema specified an inverted S-shaped wave, but in 2003 it was changed to a regular S-shape to align with the {{ill|JIS X 0213|de|JIS X 0213|ja|JIS X 0213|zh|JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|1=JIS X 0213|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6108269}} standard for kanji encoding. However, due to a printing error, maps published from November 13, 2003 already showed the regular S-shape in their legends, and published copies of the schema were corrected by pasting the regular S-shape over the original inverted S-shape.
== Origin == The origin of the onsen mark is debated.
=== Origin at Isobe Onsen === <gallery> File:磯部温泉 日本最古の温泉記号 - panoramio.jpg|Monument marking "Japan's oldest onsen symbol" (Isobe Onsen) </gallery> One theory credits {{ill|Isobe Onsen|de|Isobe-Onsen|ja|磯部温泉|zh|磯部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Onsen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Onsen|1=Isobe Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588088}} in Annaka, Gunma Prefecture, as the birthplace of the symbol<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100197482.pdf|title=群馬がいちばん〈温泉〉|publisher=群馬県|accessdate=2021-10-20}}</ref>. On March 25, 1661 (Manji 4), a government land dispute decree included a map showing a symbol resembling the modern onsen mark at "Nishi-Isobe Shio-no-Kubo," the present site of Isobe Onsen. This document was rediscovered around 1970 and commemorated with a monument in 1981{{Sfn|外山健一|2009|p=2}}<ref name="朝日20210731"/>. In 2016, Isobe Onsen celebrated February 22 as Onsen Mark Day (the numeral 2 resembling steam) and opposed the 2016 symbol revision as the purported origin site<ref name="朝日20210731"/>.
=== German origin theory === Another theory suggests the symbol was adopted from a 19th-century {{ill|Germany|en|Germany|qid=Q183|lt=German|1=Germany}} map symbol by Japanese surveyors of the Army General Staff's Land Survey Department<ref name="朝日20210731"/>.
=== Origin at Beppu Onsen discredited === A tale credits entrepreneur Kumahachi Aburaya with creating the symbol based on a human palm print when he settled in {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu|1=Beppu}} in 1911. However, because the onsen mark appeared on maps as early as 1884, this story is untenable<ref>「得ダネひとかじり」知ってる？ルーツ「温泉マーク 油屋熊八考案説」『日本経済新聞』2013年2月2日</ref>. Aburaya did heavily use the mark in promoting Beppu Onsen{{Sfn|外山健一|2009|p=4}}.
== Pictogram == Under {{ill|JIS Z 8210|de|JIS Z 8210|ja|JIS Z 8210|zh|JIS Z 8210|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS Z 8210|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS Z 8210|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS Z 8210|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, two designs are defined for "hot spring" signage; displayers may choose either: * No. 5.4.9a: similar to the map symbol but with three steam curves of equal length. * No. 5.4.9b: the onsen mark accompanied by three bathing figures, adopted by ISO 7001 as "Hot spring or hot tub" (PITC 013) and added to JIS in July 2017.
== Derived symbols == <gallery> File:静岡県熱海市市章.svg|City emblem of {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|qid=Q653364|lt=Atami|1=Atami}}, Shizuoka Prefecture </gallery> * The {{ill|city emblem|de|Stadtwappen|ja|市章|zh|市徽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city emblem|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city emblem|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city emblem|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市町村章|1=municipal emblem of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11480641}} of Atami, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Yellow Magic Orchestra|en|Yellow Magic Orchestra|qid=Q854590|lt=Yellow Magic Orchestra|1=Yellow Magic Orchestra}}: the letters "YMO" drawn by adding three lines to the mark on the album {{ill|BGM|en|BGM|qid=Q447848|lt=BGM|1=BGM}}. * Logo of {{ill|Hauflous|de|Hauflous|ja|ハウフルス|zh|哈夫罗斯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hauflous|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hauflous|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hauflous|1=How Full's inc.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11326245}}: two eyes inside the onsen mark. * In the anime {{ill|K-On!|en|K-On!|qid=Q482482|lt=K-On!|1=K-On!}}, the band After School Tea Time adds a cup under the mark. * Emblem of {{ill|Thespa Gunma|en|Thespa Gunma|qid=Q1069071|lt=Thespakusatsu Gunma|1=Thespa Gunma}} features the steam lines above the word "KUSATSU", a legacy of the club's origins in Kusatsu.
== Code positions == {{ill|Miscellaneous Symbols|en|Miscellaneous Symbols|qid=Q180699|lt=Miscellaneous Symbols|1=Miscellaneous Symbols}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! Symbol !! {{ill|Unicode|en|Unicode|qid=Q8819|lt=Unicode|1=Unicode}} !! {{ill|JIS X 0213|de|JIS X 0213|ja|JIS X 0213|zh|JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JIS X 0213|1=JIS X 0213|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6108269}} !! {{ill|Character reference|de|Zeichencode|ja|文字参照|zh|字符引用|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Character reference|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Character reference|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Character reference|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Character entity reference|qid=Q15932945}} !! {{ill|Name|en|Name|qid=Q82799|lt=Name|1=Name}} {{CharCode|9832|2668|1-6-76|Onsen symbol}} |}
== Footnotes ==
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== Further reading == * (財)日本地図センター『地図記号のうつりかわり -地形図図式・記号の変遷-』1994年（平成6年）3月31日発行, pp.17, 126-127 * {{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|qid=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}} Heisei 14 1:25,000 Topographic Map Schema (2002) * Notice of partial amendment to the Heisei 14 1:25,000 Topographic Map Schema (2003) * (社)日本測量協会『平成14年2万5千分1地形図図式』2003年（平成15年）11月13日, pp.60-61 * {{cite journal |和書|date=2003|url=http://bud.beppu-u.ac.jp/modules/xoonips/detail.php?id=bs01712|title=「温泉マーク♨」由来記|author=外山健一|journal=別府史談会|volume=No.17|pages=73-76|accessdate=2019-12-25|naid=120001798390|ref={{SfnRef|外山健一|2009}}}} * {{cite book|author=金益見|title=性愛空間の文化史|date=2012|publisher=ミネルヴァ書房|isbn=9784623064106|ref=harv}} * 西村蹊二編『地図が全部わかる本』1979年, 日本交通公社出版事業局
== External links == * [https://hide-tabi.blog.ss-blog.jp/2008-03-15 日本最古の温泉マーク、万治四年（1661年）の裁許絵図を見る!] (巡汤狂热者日志) * [https://kokudochizu.co.jp/columnandother/membercolumnmenu/zusikika.html 国土地図株式会社, 地形図図式記号の起源と用語説明（カ行）]{{リンク切れ|date=2021-07}}
